attacking_style: Attacks 2-5km from summit on final climbs or initiates moves on descents before climbs; prefers catching fading breakaway riders in final kilometers rather than launching earlier long-range efforts.
team_tactics: Astana uses him dual-purpose: stage hunting from breakaways when out of GC, or working all day for teammates (FUGLSANG) when team has better GC card. Self-sufficient in breaks, driving pace personally.
terrain_preference: Hilly finishes with short steep climbs (5-10km) where time trial power translates to sustained pace; exposed on beyond-category mountain finishes where pure climbers accelerate repeatedly.
vulnerability: Fades on longest/highest climbs when repeatedly attacked; poor sprint finish from small groups despite reaching finale; positioning errors leading to crash involvement or being held up.
key_patterns: Wins by catching exhausted breakaway leaders in final 100-500m, not by early attacks (4 instances)
Strong time trial ability (16.4km ITT winner) enables sustained climbing pace but not explosive accelerations
Dropped when pure climbers accelerate on HC/altitude finishes despite holding wheels initially (3 observations)
Positions aggressively at front in sprint stages to protect GC rather than conserving energy
Used tactically as breakaway GC threat when close on time, forcing opponents to chase
Form peaks for week-long stage races (Dauphine, Oman wins) rather than three-week Grand Tours
form_trajectory: negative: dropped early on climbs in 2023 Tour de Suisse and faded at Amstel, indicating decline from 2021 peak (Dauphine 2nd, Tour 3rd GC)
